description: The trp operon regulates tryptophan biosynthesis through dual control mechanisms: transcriptional repression by TrpR-tryptophan complex and translational attenuation controlled by ribosome speed and tRNA availability. This system demonstrates sophisticated RNA-level regulation where the leader peptide sequence acts as a biosensor, coupling translation speed to transcription termination decisions.

name: Tryptophan Operon with Attenuation
notes: This flowchart represents the trp operon with 45 unique nodes (A-BM). Contains 3 OR logic gates (tryptophan level checks, tRNA availability, ribosome position) and 2 AND gates (attenuation requires low Trp AND ribosome stalling; full transcription requires antiterminator AND RNAP continuation). The system demonstrates dual regulation: transcriptional repression (TrpR-mediated) and translational attenuation (leader peptide-mediated). The attenuation mechanism couples translation speed to RNA secondary structure formation, creating an elegant biosensor. All mechanisms validated against genetic and structural studies.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on decades of research on the trp operon, including Charles Yanofsky's pioneering work on attenuation. The dual regulatory mechanism (repression + attenuation) and the structural basis of leader peptide regulation have been validated through genetic, biochemical, and structural studies. The attenuation mechanism is one of the most elegant examples of RNA-level gene regulation.

sources.note: Classic paper on attenuation mechanism by its discoverer
sources.title: Attenuation in the control of expression of bacterial operons

sources.note: Modern synthesis of trp operon regulation
sources.title: Regulation of the trp operon

sources.authors: Oxender DL, Zurawski G, Yanofsky C
sources.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
sources.title: The tryptophan operon: a coordinated mechanism for the regulation of biosynthesis

verifiedBy: Literature review including Yanofsky's pioneering work on attenuation
